steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $5{x}^{4}{y}^{3} \left({x}^{5}+3\right)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: Factor $5x^9y^3 + 15x^4y^3$ by pulling out the greatest common factor. Both terms share $5x^4y^3$: $5x^9y^3 + 15x^4y^3 = 5x^4y^3(x^5 + 3)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $5{x}^{4}{y}^{3} \left(3{x}^{5}\right)$: Expanding gives $15x^9y^3$, which is missing the $15x^4y^3$ term entirely. ✗
  • Choice B — $5{x}^{4}{y}^{3} \left({x}^{3}\right)$: Expanding gives $5x^7y^3$, which matches neither term of the original expression. ✗
  • Choice C — $5{x}^{4}{y}^{3} \left({x}^{5}+3\right)$: Expanding gives $5x^9y^3 + 15x^4y^3$ — exactly the original expression. ✓
  • Choice D — $5{x}^{4}{y}^{3} \left({x}^{3}+3\right)$: Expanding gives $5x^7y^3 + 15x^4y^3$, but the original has $x^9$, not $x^7$. ✗
That matches Choice C — $5{x}^{4}{y}^{3} \left({x}^{5}+3\right)$.
